call.description: Executes a command string and returns the result. Supports chaining multiple commands with semicolons.

parameters.run: Command string to execute (supports semicolon-separated commands)
parameters.options: Object with srcElement or element references

exec.description: Executes a specific function reference with arguments using dot-notation path resolution.

parameters.args: Arguments object passed to the resolved function

wait.description: Waits for a specified number of milliseconds using requestAnimationFrame before executing a callback.

parameters.callback: Function to execute after the delay

click.description: Simulates a click or double-click event on a DOM element by dispatching a CustomEvent.

parameters.dbl: If truthy, dispatches a dblclick event instead

load.description: Initializes the Front framework. Disables page visibility, sets up the window load event listener, and marks the framework as loaded.

start.description: Handles the full initialization of the application: reads script config, sets up event listeners (click, submit, keydown, mouseover, mouseout, input), and begins loading assets.

disable.description: Controls document visibility. Used internally to hide the page during load and show it once ready.

parameters.bool: If true, hides the document; if false, shows it

parse.callString.description: Parses a Front command string into a structured object with function name, element references, values, and delay settings.

parameters.options: Object with srcElement or element context

parse.attribute.description: Parses a string into key-value pairs separated by ';' and split at the first ':'.

parse.text.description: Parses an HTML string into a DOM node (spot element). Extracts html attributes, body attributes, and doctype. Optionally excludes specified tags.

parameters.exclude: Optional array of tag names to strip from the output

parse.text.returns: A DOM element containing the parsed content with htmlAttr, bodyAttr, and doctype properties

parse.json.description: Parses a JSON string safely, returning an object with value or error information.
parse.json.returns: Object with value property on success, or errorName and errorMessage on failure

element.select.description: Selects elements by CSS selector. Supports index notation with [n] suffix. Returns single element by default or array if list parameter is true.

parameters.list: If true, always returns a NodeList

element.select.returns: Single element, NodeList, or empty string if not found

element.find.description: Finds elements within a specific node using a CSS selector.

parameters.node: The parent DOM node to search within
parameters.selector: CSS selector (use '*' prefix to always return NodeList)

element.find.returns: Single element, NodeList, or empty NodeList

element.get.description: Gets the value or property of an element. Automatically resolves the correct property based on tag name (value for inputs, src for images, textContent for divs, etc.).

parameters.attrValue: Optional attribute name to get the value of
parameters.attrName: If true, returns the property name instead of the value
parameters.customValue: Custom value key (e.g. 'label' for select options)

element.set.description: Sets a value or attribute on an element. Handles different set operations like settext, sethtml, setattr, setvalue, sethref, setsrc, etc.

element.add.class.description: Adds one or more CSS classes to an element.

parameters.options: Object with element and space-separated class names as value

element.toggle.class.description: Toggles one or more CSS classes on an element.

parameters.options: Object with element and value (space-separated class names)

element.operate.description: Applies arithmetic operations (+, -, *, /) to numeric values in attributes or query parameters.

parameters.query: Query parameter name to target, or falsy to apply to entire value
parameters.attr: The attribute value string to operate on

element.getTagLink.description: Traverses up the DOM tree to find the closest anchor (<a>), button, or label ancestor.
element.getTagLink.returns: The found ancestor element or null

element.getClosestWithAttr.description: Traverses up the DOM tree to find the closest ancestor with a specific attribute.

parameters.attr: The attribute name to search for

element.getClosestWithAttr.returns: The found ancestor element or null

element.resolveCall.description: Resolves the call context of an element, parsing exec objects or value strings into a normalized call structure.

element.resolveBindingValue.description: Resolves a binding value for an element, checking for default values in {variable:default} syntax.

element.getPropertyByPath.description: Retrieves a nested property from an object using a dot-separated path.
element.getPropertyByPath.returns: The resolved value or empty string if not found

element.extractBracketValues.description: Extracts values enclosed in square brackets from a string. Handles nested brackets correctly.

parameters.str: The string to extract bracket values from

element.extractBracketValues.returns: Single string value, array of values, or empty string

element.saveOriginalValues.description: Saves the original state of an element (attributes, text, HTML, value, style) for later reset operations.

parameters.element: The element to capture original values from

element.runOnEvent.description: Checks and runs event handlers (on[event]) for an element after an attribute or action executes.

parameters.parsedCall: Object with exec property containing func and element

element.onchange.description: Handles onchange callbacks for an element by looking for 'on[attr]change' attributes.

element.onload.description: Executes onload actions for an element or NodeList. Supports module and plugin prefixed events.

parameters.value: The attribute value identifying the load event

element.onsubmit.description: Handles form submit events, executing onformsubmit attribute actions.

log.info.description: Logs information to console if debugging is enabled. Returns a function that performs the actual logging.

log.error.description: Logs errors to console if debugging is enabled. Supports predefined message codes.

parameters.v: Optional message code (100, 400) or custom prefix

log.warn.description: Logs warnings to console if debugging is enabled.

parameters.v: Optional message code or custom prefix

config.get.description: Retrieves configuration from a DOM element's conf attribute, merging with standard defaults. Supports extension-prefixed configs.

parameters.extension: Extension name prefix (or false for global config)
parameters.element: DOM element to read config attribute from

config.set.description: Applies global configuration from the script element to the app object. Sets properties like debug, varsDir, storageKey, etc.

globals.set.description: Sets a global variable accessible throughout the application.

globals.get.description: Gets the value of a global variable.

globals.refresh.description: Refreshes global variables (href, title) and updates all live-bound elements registered via bindglobal.

caches.get.description: Retrieves cached data from the specified storage mechanism (local, session, cookie, or window memory).

parameters.options: Optional object with fetchJson flag to auto-parse

caches.set.description: Stores data in the specified cache mechanism with optional format parsing and TTL.

parameters.response: Response object with responseText or data property

caches.validate.description: Validates a cached entry against its TTL. Returns the cached data if still valid, or removes expired entries.

parameters.options: Object with ttl, keyType, and storageKey

caches.validate.returns: Cached data if valid, or undefined if expired

caches.remove.description: Removes a cached entry from the specified storage mechanism.

listeners.add.description: Adds an event listener to an element, removing any existing identical listener first to prevent duplicates.

listeners.remove.description: Removes an event listener from an element.

listeners.dispatch.description: Dispatches a custom event on the window with optional detail data.

parameters.name: Custom event name
parameters.detail: Optional detail object attached to the event

listeners.change.description: Handles value change events for inputs, triggering onvaluechange, onvaluechangeif, onstatevaluechange, and onselectchange callbacks.

variables.update.attributes.description: Replaces {variable} and {variable:default} patterns in element attributes with the provided value.

parameters.options: Optional object with reset, resetSoft, reloadContent, single, exclude flags

variables.update.content.description: Replaces {variable} and {variable:default} patterns in element text nodes (content) with the provided value.

variables.reset.attributes.description: Resets all element attributes to their original values (captured by saveOriginalValues).

variables.reset.content.description: Resets element innerHTML to its original value.

querystrings.get.description: Gets a query string parameter value from a URL or the current page URL.

parameters.url: URL string to parse (or false/null to use current location)
parameters.param: The query parameter name to retrieve

querystrings.get.returns: The parameter value or empty string

templates.render.description: Renders the loaded templates into the document, resolving srcDoc and src templates into header, aside, main, and footer sections.

templates.resolveBase.description: Resolves relative src paths in HTML by prepending the base href.
templates.resolveBase.returns: HTML string with resolved absolute paths

attributes.run.description: Runs FTML (Front Text Markup Language) attribute processing on elements matching the given selector. This is the core rendering engine.

parameters.selector: CSS selector string or array of elements
parameters.exclude: Optional array of attribute names to skip

attributes.runDeferred.description: Executes deferred attributes (like clone) that depend on a fully processed DOM.

xhr.request.description: Creates and sends an XHR request with full support for caching, DOM updates, loaders, callbacks, and JSON payload construction.
xhr.request.example: app.xhr.request({ url: '/api/data', method: 'GET', cache: { mechanism: 'session', format: 'json', keyType: 'var', key: 'myData' } })

parameters.options: Configuration object with url, method, target, headers, cache, onload, success, error, loader, type, credentials, enctype, etc.

xhr.finalize.description: Checks if all assets (extensions, vars) are loaded and finalizes the app by running attributes and showing the page.

parameters.type: The asset type that just finished loading

assets.load.description: Initiates loading of all application assets. For frontpages: loads extensions and vars. For sub-pages: loads templates.

assets.set.description: Parses script element attributes to determine which modules, plugins, and vars to load.

parameters.scriptAttr: Attributes from the front.js script element

assets.get.vars.description: Loads variable JSON files from the configured varsDir. Checks session cache first before making XHR requests.

assets.get.extensions.description: Loads all modules and plugins by injecting script tags. Calls __autoload on each extension once loaded.

assets.get.templates.description: Loads template HTML files via XHR for multi-page template rendering.
